# Copilot Instructions for TDD-Bench-Verified

This is a fork of [IBM/TDD-Bench-Verified](https://github.com/IBM/TDD-Bench-Verified), a benchmark for evaluating LLM-generated test cases in a test-driven development (TDD) setting. It is derived from SWE-bench Verified.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Install the package (editable mode)
pip install -e .

# Generate the dataset (requires HuggingFace access to princeton-nlp/SWE-bench_Verified)
python dataset_preparation.py

# Run all tests
pytest tests/

# Run a single test
pytest tests/test_evaluation.py::test_make_run_report

# Run a single-instance evaluation (requires Docker)
python -m tddbench.harness.run_evaluation \
    --predictions_path gold \
    --max_workers 1 \
    --instance_ids sympy__sympy-20590 \
    --run_id validate-gold
```

## Architecture

### Dataset & Evaluation Pipeline

Each benchmark instance pairs an issue description with a codebase snapshot (`c_old`). A prediction is a set of tests that should **fail** on `c_old` and **pass** on `c_new` (the fixed version). The evaluation harness builds Docker images, runs tests inside containers, and grades results.

### `tddbench/harness/` ‚Äî Core Modules

- **`constants.py`** ‚Äî Central config: `SWEbenchInstance` TypedDict, test status enums (`TestStatus`, `ResolvedStatus`), and `MAP_REPO_VERSION_TO_SPECS` mapping repo+version pairs to installation/test specs.
- **`test_spec.py`** ‚Äî `TestSpec` dataclass that converts a dataset instance into Docker build scripts (env setup, repo install, test execution). Image keys are content-hashed so environment images are reused across instances.
- **`docker_build.py`** ‚Äî Builds a hierarchy of Docker images: base ‚Üí env ‚Üí instance. Uses `ThreadPoolExecutor` for parallel builds. Skips rebuilds when images are up-to-date unless `--force_rebuild` is set.
- **`run_evaluation.py`** ‚Äî CLI entry point (`python -m tddbench.harness.run_evaluation`). Loads predictions, builds Docker images, runs tests in containers with timeouts, collects coverage, and writes grading reports.
- **`grading.py`** ‚Äî Parses test logs using repo-specific parsers, classifies tests into fail-to-pass / pass-to-pass buckets, and computes resolution status (FULL / PARTIAL / NO).
- **`log_parsers.py`** ‚Äî Per-repo test output parsers (pytest, Django, SymPy, etc.) referenced via `MAP_REPO_TO_PARSER`.
- **`docker_utils.py`** ‚Äî Utility helpers for Docker operations (copying files into containers, executing commands with timeouts, cleanup).
- **`dockerfiles.py`** ‚Äî Dockerfile template strings for the base, env, and instance image layers.
- **`prepare_images.py`** ‚Äî Filters dataset instances and pre-builds their Docker images.
- **`remove_containers.py`** ‚Äî Removes Docker containers for specified instance IDs.
- **`utils.py`** ‚Äî Shared utilities: `load_tddbench_dataset()` for loading data from HuggingFace or local JSON, plus type helpers.

### `scripts/` ‚Äî Utility Scripts

- **`prepare_selected_dataset.py`** ‚Äî Randomly samples items from `TDD_Bench.json`. Requires `-o`/`--output` to specify the output file path. Supports `-N` for count and `--seed` for reproducibility.
- **`generate_predictions.py`** ‚Äî Orchestrates running Copilot CLI inside harness Docker containers to generate test predictions. Takes `--benchmark` (JSON) and `--variant` (YAML), outputs to `copilot/<variant>.json`. Supports `--max_workers` for parallelism and resume-on-failure.

### `variants/` ‚Äî Prompt Variants

YAML files defining `model_name` and `prompt` template (with `{problem_statement}` placeholder) for different prediction strategies.

### Key Data Files

- **`TDD_Bench.json`** ‚Äî Full dataset (449 instances), generated by `dataset_preparation.py`. Gitignored.
- **`TDD_Bench_selected.json`** ‚Äî Subset for focused evaluation. Gitignored.
- **`copilot/`** ‚Äî Generated predictions output directory. Gitignored.
- **`golden_test_patch.json`** ‚Äî Reference predictions (gold patches) used for validation.
- **`Otter/`** ‚Äî Pre-generated test predictions from the Otter model.

## Conventions

- Python ‚â• 3.11 is required.
- Docker is required for evaluation; the harness manages container lifecycle automatically.
- Predictions are JSON arrays of `{"instance_id": "...", "model_patch": "..."}` where `model_patch` is a unified diff.
- Test scripts inside Docker containers use `#!/bin/bash\nset -euxo pipefail` for strict error handling.
- Logging uses per-build file loggers (created via `setup_logger()`, closed via `close_logger()` to avoid fd leaks).
- Coverage threshold is 45% minimum (configured in `codecov.yml`).
